column11:
They asked to write a program to  sum numbers from range [1, n] given that it should not be a multiple of x and y. They gave values of n, x and y..
They asked to write a program to add adjacent numbers until we found the the numbers are not circular which means the forst number and last number are same and increase the count if the sum of those adjacent numbers is divisible by N. They gave arr[], N.
column13: MCQs, 35 which includes quantitative aptitude from all the topics, logical and  reasoning , programming, core subjects like OS, CN, Testing, DBMS and 2 Coding questions , easy and both were arrays..

column6: The online test was 80mins. We had technical mcq on some java, oops, sql queries, os, aptitude questions( profit loss, tap filling tank, ratio etc). 2 coding questions based on arrays. Each of the sections were timed.

column6: There were sections like Quantitative Aptitude , Logical Reasoning, Networking Concepts, Operating System, Programming Snippets (OOPs), 2 Coding Questions
column9:
There were two rounds of interview:
1. Technical/Managerial Interview: Asked about the projects and internships. Asked about OOPs concepts and any project example where I've used OOPs. Try being confident and have a good communication. It helps a lot.(Keep your GitHub and other sites you have mentioned in your resume...most of the time they check and an updated one is good to have)
2. HR Interview: Asked about my Open Source Contribution. Asked personal questions and HR questions. Try doing some certifications(Try doing reputed ones...I had Azure AI-900 certification, it gave an edge)
